name: Auvergne-Rhone-Alpes (France)

colors.symbolism: the field, drawn from the gules shared by Auvergne and Lyonnais
the saltire dividing the four quarters
the azure of the Dauphine quarter
the gold of the lion of Lyonnais and of the gonfalon of Auvergne
outlines and details of the heraldic charges

description: A red field divided per saltire by a white saltire, with the four triangular quarters each bearing the historic arms of one of the constituent provinces of the merged region.
history: Auvergne-Rhone-Alpes is a French region created by the territorial reform of 2016, merging the former regions of Auvergne and Rhone-Alpes. France traditionally does not promote regional flags, and this composite armorial banner is used in a de-facto capacity rather than as an officially adopted regional flag.

features.symbolism: A red field divided per saltire by a white saltire, with the four quarters charged with the historic arms of the constituent provinces of the merged region.

features.role: the historic arms of Auvergne, Lyonnais, Dauphine, and Forez/Bourbonnais arranged in the four quarters
features.symbolism: Each quarter bears the arms of one of the historic provinces brought together by the 2016 merger that created Auvergne-Rhone-Alpes.
